description_long: This object consists of two knives, each featuring a broad, slightly curved blade with a visibly weathered, matte metallic finish. The blades are single-edged and show signs of wear, with some discoloration and roughness, suggesting frequent use or age. Both knives have handles made of wood, shaped ergonomically with a gentle curve for a comfortable grip, and are fastened with visible metal rivets. The handles display a natural wood grain pattern and a medium brown color, contrasting with the darker, mottled steel of the blades. There are no visible guards or crossguards between the blade and handle, and the knives lack any decorative ornamentation. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for practical use in kitchens, workshops, or urban survival scenarios.
description: A pair of weathered, single-edged knives with broad, matte steel blades and curved wooden handles secured by rivets. The design is utilitarian and shows signs of frequent use.
description_view_0: Side view showing both knives' full profiles, highlighting the broad, worn blades and curved wooden handles with visible rivets.
description_view_1: Top-down view displaying the narrow width of the blades and the flat, rectangular shape of the wooden handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the other side of the knives with the same blade and handle features.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the thinness of the blades and the alignment of the handles, with the blade edges barely visible.
attribute_blade_type: broad, single-edged, slightly curved, plain edge
attribute_handle_design: curved wooden handle with visible grain and metal rivets, ergonomic shape
